hard.next_step: JRA 6/29: Meeting set for 7/13 with Dir. of Procurement and CPO to discuss and do a brief demonstration on intake + sourcing. Internal prep call on 6/6.
hard.ais_why:
• Justin invited Tufts contacts Nicole and Alex to the Agentic AI Procurement Summit — the engagement is being driven through Zycus' agentic AI event.
• Tufts' stated mission of tackling complex global challenges through research aligns naturally with agentic-AI positioning.
• Sustained AI cadence over four months from January through the May summit invite, though there's no Avoma transcript to confirm the sentiment.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / do-nothing (fragmented PeopleSoft + Unimarket + Jagger stack, or an AI overlay on it)

north_star_verdict.headline: Re-engaged after an 8.5-month gap: a first CPO/Director-of-Procurement demo lands 13 July, but this Qualified higher-ed deal has a 28 October close that the buyer's own stated 12-18-month sourcing timeline cannot support. The deal is live, the date is not.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 July 2026: close 28 October 2026 is 114 days out. Created and qualification-pathed from 21 November 2025; StageName moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 20 January 2026, so ~167 days in Qualified with no submission-date fields populated. Close date has slipped five times (20 Mar to 23 Apr to 27 May to 29 Jul to 28 Oct), all pushed forward by Justin Ajmo, most recently 29 June 2026. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 25 September 2025 discovery call (~284 days ago); rep re-engaged 4 June 2026, buyer replied, and a 13 July 2026 intake+sourcing meeting is now booked (LastActivityDate 13 July 2026). No formal evaluation, shortlist, or vendor-selected gate reached. To close in 114 days from Qualified would require demo, RFI/RFP, shortlist, commercials, negotiation, and contract inside ~16 weeks, against a higher-ed enterprise motion that runs 12-15 months and a buyer who on 25 September 2025 said a sourcing tool is desired 'within the next 12 to 18 months' with broader platform change over '1 to 3 years'. Pace required to hold the date is unrealistic; the date will slip, the deal itself is progressing.
evidence: Deal is live and re-engaging: rep re-engaged 4 June 2026 after an 8.5-month silence, buyer replied, and a first meeting with the Chief Procurement Officer and Director of Procurement for an intake+sourcing demo is booked for 13 July 2026 (Next Step 29 June 2026; LastActivityDate 13 July 2026).

The date is the only real problem: close 28 October 2026 is 114 days out in Qualified, but Nicole Lanoue said on 25 September 2025 a sourcing tool is wanted 'within the next 12 to 18 months' and full-platform change over '1 to 3 years', so the forecast date is 6-9 months ahead of the buyer's own horizon and has already slipped five times.

Pipeline is the honest, defensible category: the deal is engaged but early (Qualified, no demo delivered, CPO not yet met, no pricing, no validated budget), so it is not Best Case; the 13 July meeting is the event that could firm it up.

metrics.narrative: Nicole Lanoue articulated clear operational pain but no quantified value case on the 25 September 2025 discovery call. She described a fragmented stack ('all requisitioning happens within Peoplesoft... we use Jagger, but only for contracts... Unimarket is what we use for our b to b marketplace') and no sourcing tool at all, and she flagged manual savings tracking ('99% of the time someone is going to tell you they track their procurement savings in an Excel spreadsheet, and it is messy and cumbersome... at Tufts, we do it the same way'). She also confirmed two open team vacancies (a contracts person and a facilities sourcing person), signalling a stretched team. None of this is quantified: no sourcing-event volume, cycle time, savings target, or cost-of-manual-work figure exists. Quantifying the pain with the Director of Procurement at the 13 July meeting (annual sourcing events run manually, cycle time, the cost of the two vacancies) is the move to convert this to a business case.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue
SF Next_Step__c 29 June 2026 (CPO + Director of Procurement meeting 13 July)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the Nicole reports to: on 25 September 2025 Nicole said she would 'make mention to my chief procurement officer that we had this introductory call and see what her appetite is... that will help me determine what the next steps should be', explicitly placing appetite and direction with the CPO. The SF Next_Step__c confirms the CPO is joining the 13 July 2026 meeting, so the role is real and mapped, but the CPO has never engaged Zycus directly and is only named in the transcript (no Salesforce contact record), so her identity and title are unverified in Salesforce and no budget authority should be asserted beyond the role. Nicole added the CPO 'comes from industry... does not come from higher ed', a useful positioning cue. Budget is a stated constraint ('budgets are limited in higher ed... a lot of it comes down to our budget'). The 13 July meeting is the first EB touch; the move is to get the CPO to name the priority and a target date in her own words.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are inferable from Nicole's 25 September 2025 reactions rather than a formal requirements doc. She responded strongly to Zycus being organically built rather than acquired ('the way that Jagger is built... it is very apparent... certain modules work in a different way'), valued a unified single platform over fragmented systems, was drawn to AI/agentic sourcing (the ANA autonomous negotiation agent and the Merlin Teams buying agent) and to guided buying for Tufts' decentralized purchasing, and repeatedly probed two hard criteria Zycus did not close out: genuine higher-ed sector fit ('what piece of the pie is higher education') and integrated savings/cost-avoidance tracking. Price sits alongside as a constraint. These are confirmed as interests, not yet ranked or written into an RFP; formalizing them with the CPO and Director of Procurement is the next step.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue

decision_process.narrative: The process is early and higher-ed-decentralized. Nicole is the evaluator and gatekeeper who screens vendors and then briefs the CPO to gauge appetite; she said on 25 September 2025 'myself and my co directors are looking at what we're doing... I do think there will be some evolvement over the next one to 3 years'. The 13 July 2026 meeting is the first time the CPO and Director of Procurement enter directly, which is a real step up in seniority. There is no formal RFP, shortlist, or scoring process yet, and Nicole herself said 'I'm not quite sure what our next step should be', so the process is being formed, not run. Reading the sequence: Nicole (Sep 2025) then a long gap then CPO + Director of Procurement (Jul 2026) shows the deal is only now reaching the people who set direction.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue
SF field history (StageName Initial Interest to Qualified 20 January 2026)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics exist yet, which is correct for a Qualified deal with no demo delivered: no MSA/SOW discussion, no procurement channel, no legal or InfoSec review, no partner/SI, and no contract-expiration forcing function on record. Tufts is a US private university, so a purchase order may or may not gate invoicing, but nothing is known. Visibility will come only after a scope and priority are agreed with the CPO; the near-term move is not paper but to establish which module leads and on what timeline, from which the paper path follows.
sources: SF Opportunity fields (no paper-process values populated)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and buyer-stated. On 25 September 2025 Nicole Lanoue described a fragmented, manual environment: requisitions and payment/reconciliation in 'Peoplesoft from 1985', a separate Unimarket marketplace, and Jagger used only for contracts, with 'no starting landing page' tying it together. She called procurement highly decentralized ('each department has a business administrator... they've already probably done their sourcing... we're almost more of a tactical buying arm') and named savings tracking as an unsolved, Excel-bound problem. Two open vacancies compound the load. The pain owners are Nicole and the Director of Procurement. It is confirmed and qualitative; what is missing is the numbers, not the pain.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue

champion.narrative: Nicole Lanoue (Grants and Finance Manager, the sole Salesforce contact role, Evaluator, primary) is a developing champion, not yet a strong one. On 25 September 2025 she engaged deeply for the full call, articulated pain and fit, was drawn to Merlin/ANA, and volunteered an internal ally: 'Mike, who came from CBRE, said he used Zycus for sourcing and contracts for 5 years... it was slick'. She committed to brief the CPO and gauge appetite. What is missing is active advocacy: after that call she went quiet for 8.5 months until the rep re-engaged on 4 June 2026, she has not made an internal introduction, built a business case, or created urgency, and she owns evaluation but not budget. She is engaged and responsive (she agreed to the 13 July meeting) but reactive. Securing her and converting the meeting into a written next step, plus activating Mike, is the champion play.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue
Rep re-engagement 4 June 2026; Next Step 29 June 2026 (meeting 13 July)

competition.narrative: The strongest current competitor is the buyer's own status quo / do-nothing path: on 25 September 2025 Nicole framed the choice as 'do we wanna consolidate systems, do we want to use AI to almost have a wrapper, a GUI layer on top of our existing systems', i.e. keep PeopleSoft + Unimarket + Jagger and bolt AI on rather than replace. Coupa is in the set but priced out in the buyer's eyes ('I've talked to Coupa before... the price tag is wild'); Justin positioned Zycus 'slightly below Coupa'. Jagger is the contracts-only incumbent and is 'big in the higher ed space', so it is the reference point but not a full-suite rival. SAP Ariba appears only as a pricing benchmark, not an active evaluation. The wedge is the organically-built unified platform plus Merlin/ANA agentic sourcing, sized and phased to a higher-ed budget so the do-nothing/AI-overlay option looks like the more expensive path over time.
sources: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue

deal_movement.summary: Deal read from field history and the buyer touch record: a long stall broken by a fresh rep-led re-engagement. Close date pushed forward five times (Mar to Apr to May to Jul to Oct), all by Justin Ajmo, most recently 29 June 2026; stage moved to Qualified on 20 January 2026; the buyer went quiet from the 25 September 2025 discovery call until the June 2026 re-engagement, and a first CPO/Director-of-Procurement meeting is now set for 13 July 2026. Momentum is rep-driven, not buyer-pulled.

items.change: Close date pushed 29 July to 28 October 2026 (fifth forward slip; owner Justin Ajmo)
Meeting booked with the CPO and Director of Procurement for a 13 July intake+sourcing demo, first senior-buyer touch
Rep re-engaged the account after an 8.5-month silence; buyer replied and agreed to meet
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified
Discovery call: fragmented-stack pain, AI interest, conflicting 12-18-month / 1-3-year timelines, CPO named as the appetite-setter

competitive_position.summary: The live threat is not a rival vendor, it is Tufts doing nothing or building an AI wrapper on the existing PeopleSoft/Unimarket/Jagger stack, framed by budget constraint (25 September 2025). Coupa was evaluated by Nicole at a prior employer and dismissed on price ('the price tag is wild'), so it ranks low as a current threat despite the brand. Jagger is the contracts-only incumbent and the higher-ed reference point but not a full-suite competitor. SAP Ariba is only a pricing benchmark. There is no active bake-off; the win path is displacing inertia with a phased, budget-sized sourcing-first entry led by Merlin/ANA.

competitors.name: Status quo / do-nothing (fragmented PeopleSoft P2P + Unimarket + Jagger contracts, or an AI wrapper over them)
competitors.quote: do we wanna consolidate systems, do we want to use AI to almost have a wrapper, a GUI layer on top of our existing systems
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue
competitors.how_we_win: Make the unified organically-built platform plus Merlin/ANA the cheaper-over-time path; phase a sourcing-first entry to fit a higher-ed budget so 'do nothing' looks like the costlier option.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: I've talked to Coupa before... and it was slick. It was nice. But the price tag is wild
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Lean on Zycus pricing below Coupa and modular scope; the buyer already self-selected Coupa out on cost.

competitors.name: Jagger (Jaggaer, contracts-only incumbent)
competitors.quote: we use Jagger, but we only use Jagger for their contracts module... Jagger has been big in the higher ed space
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus' seamless unified data flow against Jagger's visible acquisition seams that Nicole herself called out; offer to absorb contracts into one suite rather than another bolt-on.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: where would you say that Zycus falls in line when we talk about Coupa, SAP, Jagger, Ariba
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue; SF Competitors__c
competitors.how_we_win: Only a benchmark name; position Zycus as lower-complexity and lower-cost than SAP for a lean higher-ed team.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Directional fit is good on platform architecture and AI, but two buyer-raised criteria are exposed and unanswered: proven higher-ed focus and integrated savings tracking. Budget is the recurring constraint.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded higher-ed deal being re-warmed; the win levers are getting to power, beating inertia on budget terms, and de-risking change management.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Nicole: use the 13 July meeting to earn direct sponsorship from the CPO and Director of Procurement, since Nicole is the only mapped contact today.
Beat do-nothing on budget: propose a phased, sourcing-first scope sized to a higher-ed budget so the AI-wrapper/keep-PeopleSoft option looks costlier over time.
Activate Mike (ex-CBRE, five-year Zycus user at Tufts) as an internal reference to neutralize higher-ed-fit doubt and change-management risk.

items.name: Nicole Lanoue
items.title: Grants and Finance Manager
items.risk: Developing, reactive champion and the only Salesforce contact; engaged and articulate on 25 September 2025 but went quiet 8.5 months and has made no internal intro or business case. Single-thread dependency until the CPO and Director engage.

champion_strength.summary: Nicole Lanoue is a developing champion: engaged, articulate on pain and fit, and the source of an internal reference (Mike), but reactive rather than driving. She went quiet for 8.5 months after the 25 September 2025 call and only re-engaged when the rep pushed on 4 June 2026; she has made no internal introduction, built no business case, and owns evaluation but not budget. She is the only mapped contact, so the deal is single-threaded until the 13 July meeting brings the CPO and Director of Procurement in. Strength grows by converting that meeting into a written next step and activating Mike as a second voice.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025; SF OpportunityContactRole; rep re-engagement 4 June 2026, buyer reply 5 June 2026; Next Step 29 June 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is well positioned on AI narrative: Justin led with the organically-built platform and the three agents (ANA autonomous negotiation, Merlin Teams buying agent, contract AI), and Nicole engaged with genuine curiosity. The gap is not positioning but validation, no CPO has seen it and no proof point is tied to Tufts' savings-tracking or higher-ed needs yet.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 (Moderate) matches the calls: Nicole is exploring AI ('is there a way that AI could help us') and was intrigued by ANA and the Merlin Teams agent, but there is no urgency, no budget, and no senior AI sponsor yet, so it is curiosity, not appetite. The May Agentic AI Summit invite sustains the AI cadence.

items.detail: Close date 28 October 2026 is 6-9 months ahead of the buyer's own 12-18-month sourcing horizon; five prior forward slips.
Higher-ed budget constraint repeatedly raised; no budget line or approval identified.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue

items.detail: Primary threat is inertia: keep the fragmented PeopleSoft/Unimarket/Jagger stack or add an AI wrapper rather than replace; decentralized campus buying makes adoption hard.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 25 September 2025, Nicole Lanoue

items.detail: The CPO who sets appetite and budget has never engaged Zycus; deal is single-threaded through Nicole until 13 July.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is warming but rep-driven: a fresh reply and an imminent 13 July senior meeting offset a history of an 8.5-month silence and five close-date slips. Not cooling right now, but not buyer-pulled either.
items: Buyer replied to rep re-engagement and agreed to meet (5 June 2026).
First CPO/Director-of-Procurement meeting booked for 13 July 2026.
History of an 8.5-month gap and five forward close-date slips tempers confidence.

items.action: Run the 13 July intake+sourcing demo and win the CPO's appetite for a sourcing-first phase.
items.trigger: Meeting set with CPO and Director of Procurement for a brief intake+sourcing demonstration
items.expected_effect: Puts Merlin/ANA in front of the budget owner and turns a stalled Qualified deal into a directed evaluation.

items.action: Get the CPO to name the lead module and a target date, and capture it in a written next step.
items.trigger: Buyer's own 12-18-month sourcing horizon vs an unvalidated 28 October close
items.expected_effect: Replaces conflicting timelines with a buyer-owned date and firms the forecast.

items.action: Quantify Tufts' manual-sourcing and savings-tracking pain with the Director of Procurement.
items.trigger: No quantified value case; savings tracked in Excel, two open team vacancies
items.expected_effect: Builds the ROI baseline needed to justify spend against a tight higher-ed budget.

items.action: Ask Nicole to introduce Mike, the ex-CBRE Zycus user, as an internal reference.
items.trigger: Nicole named Mike as a five-year Zycus user who found it 'slick'
items.expected_effect: Adds a second internal voice and neutralizes higher-ed-fit and change-management doubt.

items.action: Propose a phased, budget-sized sourcing-first scope and reset the close date to match.
items.trigger: Higher-ed budget constraint plus a close date well ahead of the buyer's stated horizon
items.expected_effect: Makes the deal credible and beats the do-nothing/AI-wrapper alternative on cost-over-time.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A re-warmed but early Qualified higher-ed deal: a developing champion (Nicole Lanoue) has finally lined up a first CPO/Director-of-Procurement demo on 13 July, but the real contest is against Tufts' own do-nothing/AI-wrapper inertia on a constrained budget, and the 28 October close is unrealistic against the buyer's stated 12-18-month sourcing horizon.

win_position.text: We stand early and unproven: Nicole Lanoue is engaged and Zycus is well positioned on the unified platform plus Merlin/ANA, but no economic buyer has met us and the biggest rival is Tufts keeping its PeopleSoft/Unimarket/Jagger stack (Avoma, 25 Jun 2025-09-25).
The 13 July demo is the first time the Chief Procurement Officer who owns budget and appetite enters the deal, which is the single event that could lift our position (Next Step, 29 Jun 2026).

Coupa was self-selected out on price and Jagger is only the contracts incumbent, so no vendor is beating us, but inertia and a tight higher-ed budget are, and Nicole flagged 'budgets are limited in higher ed' (Avoma, 25 Sep 2025).
We are single-threaded through a reactive champion who went quiet for 8.5 months; if the CPO does not buy in on 13 July there is no path to power (Avoma, 25 Sep 2025; rep re-engagement 4 Jun 2026).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum just turned positive after a long stall: the rep re-engaged 4 June, Nicole replied 5 June, and a senior demo is booked for 13 July (Next Step, 29 Jun 2026).
It is rep-pushed not buyer-pulled, and the close date has been slipped forward five times (to 28 Oct on 29 Jun 2026), signalling repeated optimism rather than real progress (SF field history).
There has been no buyer call for ~9 months and no demo delivered yet, so engagement is still only a generic intro-level touch (Avoma last call 25 Sep 2025).

customer_commitment.text: Nicole committed to brief her CPO and has now delivered that, bringing the CPO and Director of Procurement to the 13 July meeting (Avoma, 25 Sep 2025; Next Step, 29 Jun 2026).

Commitment is still exploratory: Nicole said 'I'm not quite sure what our next step should be' and framed change over '1 to 3 years', with no budget, RFP, or decision process in place (Avoma, 25 Sep 2025).

deal_risk.text: The close date is the headline risk: 28 October 2026 sits 6-9 months inside the buyer's own 12-18-month sourcing timeline, so the date will slip (Avoma 25 Sep 2025; SF field history).
Do-nothing is the live threat, not a competitor: Tufts may keep its fragmented stack or add an AI wrapper, and no quantified savings case yet exists to justify the spend (Avoma, 25 Sep 2025).

champion_strength.evidence: Developing, reactive champion who went silent 8.5 months and owns evaluation not budget.

engagement.evidence: No buyer call in ~9 months; momentum is rep-pushed, five close-date slips.

timeline_risk.evidence: 28 Oct close is far ahead of the buyer's stated 12-18-month sourcing horizon.

customer_preference.evidence: Nicole engaged positively with the platform and Merlin/ANA and dismissed Coupa on price ('the price tag is wild'), but gave no decisive lean and deferred to her CPO.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (fragmented manual stack, Excel savings tracking, two vacancies) but no quantified savings, cycle time, or ROI.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 14 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 48 (roughly flat): Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 6 is anchored on the stage win (14), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 48, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: A re-warmed but early Qualified higher-ed deal; a developing champion (Nicole Lanoue) has finally lined up a first CPO/Director-of-Procurement demo on 13 July, but the real contest is against Tufts' own do-nothing/AI-wrapper inertia on a constrained budget, and the 28 October close is unrealistic against the buyer's stated 12-18-month sourcing horizon

The 13 July demo is the first time the Chief Procurement Officer who owns budget and appetite enters the deal, which is the single event that could lift our position (Next Step, 29 Jun 2026)

blocks.title: What moves it forward

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Field history to 2026-01-05; activities last 120d; contact roles; Q1/Q2/Q3 fields
gaps: No buyer call in the last ~9 months; latest substantive transcript is 25 September 2025, so current sentiment and the CPO's stance are unconfirmed until 13 July.
The Chief Procurement Officer and Director of Procurement are named by role only (Next_Step__c / transcript), with no Salesforce contact records, so their identities and titles are unverified.
No quantified business case (savings baseline, sourcing volume, cycle time) exists.
